steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both the clause before the blank and the clause after it are independent clauses. The correct choice must properly separate two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Nation, in: leaves only commas between two independent clauses, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — Nation. In: splits the two independent clauses into two complete sentences, satisfying the rule that independent clauses must be separated by a period, semicolon, or comma with a coordinating conjunction. ✓
  • Choice C — Nation and in: joins two independent clauses with a coordinating conjunction but lacks the comma needed before "and." ✗
  • Choice D — Nation in: runs the second independent clause into the first without proper punctuation. ✗
B is correct.
